tweets.text: I agree and Warsh hinted numerous times at understanding this bifurcated economy, something Powell largely avoided. He also vaguely mentioned 'rebalancing' via their use of rate vs balance sheet transmission mechanism and the unevenness that has on different groups. Tightening balance sheet via reducing footprint in Treasury market and duration of portfolio would do a lot more for all of the Fed's goals than 25 or 50 bps in hikes.

thread_tweets.text: I agree and Warsh hinted numerous times at understanding this bifurcated economy, something Powell largely avoided. thread_tweets.text: Agreed fiscal is a bigger driver of the inflation, but reducing long end accommodation would put a better natural check on that and a steeper yield curve would help banks/main street/real economy and take some of the support away from mega cap corporates (tech). Most of it requires Treasury cooperation.
thread_tweets.full_text: Agreed fiscal is a bigger driver of the inflation, but reducing long end accommodation would put a better natural check on that and a steeper yield curve would help banks/main street/real economy and take some of the support away from mega cap corporates (tech). tweets.full_text:
I agree and Warsh hinted numerous times at understanding this bifurcated economy, something Powell largely avoided. He also vaguely mentioned 'rebalancing' via their use of rate vs balance sheet transmission mechanism and the unevenness that has on different groups. Tightening balance sheet via reducing footprint in Treasury market and duration of portfolio would do a lot more for all of the Fed's goals than 25 or 50 bps in hikes.

Agreed fiscal is a bigger driver of the inflation, but reducing long end accommodation would put a better natural check on that and a steeper yield curve would help banks/main street/real economy and take some of the support away from mega cap corporates (tech). Most of it requires Treasury cooperation.

tweets.text:
This is the bull case for onchain collectible platforms I've been preaching about since the day the digital repack mechanism was invented.

Midcurvers have been FUDing these platforms as just iterated casino platforms for months now, but haven't been able to see the bigger picture that has now started to unfold:

It's incredibly hard to start new marketplaces from scratch: no liquidity = no users = no network effects. 

However, Gachas and Claws have been the best imaginable catalyst and flywheel to facilitate tokenizing a tremendous amount of cards and bringing them onchain to overcome this liquidity gap.

It seems we have now hit a critical level of liquidity where users are finally attracted to these marketplaces and network effects are starting to take place.

Let this continue for a few more months and we're going to hit an inflection point where sourcing onchain becomes so much better that offchain collectors will sooner or later be forced to come onchain.

We are only just getting started and won't be done before we have onboarded all the collectors from eBay, Goldin and similarly extractive and outdated inefficient platforms to @Collector_Crypt and @Beezie.

The future of collecting is onchain.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Apart from consistent growth in Gachapon spending, secondary marketplace activity on @Collector_Crypt has also been gaining traction since the launch of its native marketplace in late April. Users are buying, selling and trading slabs on the marketplace.
 
When P2P OTC trade https://t.co/yApDfImcGg

tweets.text:
I believe we are currently on the brink. We are entering a particularly risky period expected between the 2026 midterm election and the 2028 presidential election. At the same time, the monetary situation is becoming increasingly threatening. The U.S. government currently spends $7 trillion while only taking in approximately $5 trillion, resulting in 40% overspending. While the government has a significant amount of debt to finance, the demand for that debt is falling. This decline in demand is driven by standard supply and demand factors, as well as fears from debt holders regarding potential sanctions.
#geopolitics @edels0n

tweets.text:
I have written in my STRC article that there were roughly 4 main variables for demand in STRC:

- Credit risk (depends on MSTR leverage ratio and BTC price)
- STRC yield / dividend rate
- Awareness around the instrument
- Confidence

Right now we're seeing the confidence lever getting hurt, and it's the variable that has the quickest impact on STRC price.

I'm not of the opinion that 'once confidence is lost, it's over', because expecting STRC not to trade below $99 was stupid from the beginning, and it's definitely not necessary to hold this view to buy it.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
New article on STRC.

I break down:
- how the mechanism works
- why it can scale massively
- what the real risks are

https://t.co/UuUM4s0CqN
tweets.full_text:
I have written in my STRC article that there were roughly 4 main variables for demand in STRC:

- Credit risk (depends on MSTR leverage ratio and BTC price)
- STRC yield / dividend rate
- Awareness around the instrument
- Confidence

Right now we're seeing the confidence lever getting hurt, and it's the variable that has the quickest impact on STRC price.

I'm not of the opinion that 'once confidence is lost, it's over', because expecting STRC not to trade below $99 was stupid from the beginning, and it's definitely not necessary to hold this view to buy it.

Also basic features like timers etc for working out.

tweets.text:
That's why I'm excited to receive my Surface Laptop Ultra. It has a 20-core NVIDIA Grace CPU and an NVIDIA Blackwell RTX.

I suspect a lot of AI inference will run locally in the near future: voice commands, grammar checking, real-time transcription, meeting summaries, coding assistance, semantic search across files, image generation and editing, translation, and personalized copilots that don't need to send every request to the cloud.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: We've gone really quickly from "local models are dogshit" to "local models are good actually" (like, a 12 month window from A to B). I don't think they're actually good ENOUGH yet. We need an Opus 4.5 quality local model. When that happens, I think the world will spill over.
tweets.full_text:
That's why I'm excited to receive my Surface Laptop Ultra. It has a 20-core NVIDIA Grace CPU and an NVIDIA Blackwell RTX.

I suspect a lot of AI inference will run locally in the near future: voice commands, grammar checking, real-time transcription, meeting summaries, coding assistance, semantic search across files, image generation and editing, translation, and personalized copilots that don't need to send every request to the cloud.
